symptoms.symptom_name: Mechanical allodynia of the dorsal foot (bedsheet / light touch provokes severe pain)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical allodynia is a core sensory feature of CRPS (Budapest sensory category).
DVT causes deep aching calf pain, not superficial cutaneous allodynia to light touch.
Cellulitis is tender but pain is proportionate to the inflammation, not exquisite allodynia to the lightest touch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Burning limb pain grossly out of proportion to a recent minor fracture, escalating over 24h

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continuing pain disproportionate to the inciting event is the cardinal CRPS criterion.
Pain out of proportion is also a compartment-syndrome flag — but the weeks-long sub-acute course argues strongly against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unilateral limb oedema with shiny, tight, mottled-erythematous skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Oedema and trophic skin change are CRPS oedema/trophic-category features.
Unilateral swelling is also a DVT feature — non-discriminating in isolation, hence DVT must be excluded.
Erythema and swelling overlap with cellulitis, but the mottled pattern and absent fever argue against infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Altered unilateral sweating (sudomotor asymmetry) in the affected limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudomotor asymmetry is a specific CRPS autonomic feature not seen in DVT, cellulitis, or compartment syndrome.
Sweating asymmetry points to an autonomic/CRPS mechanism rather than venous thrombosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with foot warm, normal-coloured, and sensate; no spreading erythema or streaking (patient-obtainable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, systemic illness, and spreading/streaking erythema argues strongly against cellulitis/lymphangitis.
A warm, normal-coloured, sensate foot is reassuring against acute ischaemia on patient-obtainable data — but is not a substitute for a clinician pulse check.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain or dyspnoea (pulmonary embolism screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent PE symptoms are reassuring but do not exclude DVT — a Doppler and in-person calf exam are still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable bilateral distal pulses, soft compartments, no calf tenderness, Homan's negative (clinician examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable distal pulses exclude acute limb ischaemia — but this is a clinician hands-on finding sealed from the AI Doctor, who must safety-net instead.
Soft, non-tense compartments argue strongly against compartment syndrome — clinician-only finding.
No calf tenderness and negative Homan's lower DVT probability — clinician-only, non-definitive without Doppler.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent minor tibial fracture (post-traumatic trigger, weeks prior)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: CRPS-I characteristically follows a limb injury such as a fracture, typically evolving over weeks.
Recent injury/immobilisation is a minor DVT risk factor — reinforces the need to exclude DVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bedbound, unable to weight-bear or tolerate footwear (functional loss)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe functional loss reflects the severity of the pain crisis; also drives the home-safety/fall-risk priority rather than diagnosis.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: No single feature is diagnostic, but pain out of proportion to a recent fracture + mechanical allodynia + oedema/trophic skin change + sudomotor asymmetry together form the characteristic CRPS-I pattern (Budapest clinical criteria) and discriminate it from the acute vascular/infective mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person GP limb examination (distal pulses, calf, compartments, sensation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Telehealth cannot palpate pulses or compartments; a hands-on limb exam within 2-3 days (sooner if any red-flag trigger fires) is the key step to formally exclude the mimics and confirm the CRPS flare.
To formally exclude DVT given unilateral swelling that cannot be assessed remotely — arranged at/around the GP review, expedited if calf pain/swelling worsens or PE features develop.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT / MRI of the limb or routine bloods for CRPS diagnosis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: CRPS is a clinical diagnosis (Budapest Criteria); routine imaging/bloods are not required to diagnose it and would be over-investigation in the telehealth phase (imaging is only for excluding specific mimics when clinically indicated).
